language_stack.framework: Cargo workspace (resolver=3) + Tokio async runtime + serde/serde_json contracts + thiserror error types + VitePress docs (Bun)

hygiene_evidence.README: README.md (6622B, with badges, status block, quick-start, design principles)
hygiene_evidence.CHANGELOG: CHANGELOG.md (546B, Keep-a-Changelog with [Unreleased] entry referencing audit #206)
hygiene_evidence.CI: .github/workflows/ (17 workflows: ci, lint, quality-gate, journey-gate, codeql, codeql-rust, scorecard, secrets-scan, fr-coverage, doc-links, pages, release, cargo-audit, cargo-deny, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, fr-coverage)
hygiene_evidence.DEPENDABOT: .github/dependabot.yml (cargo, gomod, npm, pip, github-actions — weekly, grouped)
hygiene_evidence.CONTRIBUTING: CONTRIBUTING.md (1231B, branch naming + conventional commits + PR checklist)
hygiene_evidence.SECURITY: SECURITY.md (867B, GitHub Security Advisories + 48h SLA + scanning matrix)

dup_exposure: phenotype-bus / phenotype-event-bus (event bus — pheno-nats could re-export rather than re-implement publisher traits)
phenotype-request-id (request/correlation ID propagation for adapter calls; required for trace stitching)
phenotype-registry (service-name resolution for adapter endpoints — pheno-nats/pheno-minio hand-roll URL config today)
phenotype-dep-guard (org-wide cargo-deny/audit/machete/semver-checks policy bundle — repo has its own .golangci.yml/deny.toml; would centralize)
phenotype-errors / phenotype-error-core (canonical error taxonomy; thiserror wrappers in each crate diverge today)
phenotype-port-interfaces from phenoShared (already referenced in README; not actually declared in any crate's Cargo.toml)
phenotype-postfx (post-effect runner that could host retry/circuit-breaker logic missing from adapters)
phenotype-tooling / phenotype.just (shared just recipes — repo has its own minimal justfile; could extend the org library variant)
phenotype-journeys (journey primitives — repo has journey-gate workflow but no FR/journey-traceable tests)
phenotype-llm-routing ports in PhenoObservability (phenotype-llm crate re-implements routing traits that already exist in observability-ports)
sota_gaps: Daemon not implemented — src/main.rs:1 is a `println!` placeholder; the 'runtime' crate name is aspirational, not actual
No crate publish path — release.yml exists but no crates.io publish step, no provenance attestation, no per-crate versioning
No OpenTelemetry / structured tracing — adapters log via println/eprintln only; no #[instrument], no OTel exporter, no span propagation across pheno-nats -> phenotype-llm
No retry/circuit-breaker/backoff — every adapter (pheno-nats, pheno-minio, phenotype-surrealdb, phenotype-llm) opens raw connections with no resilience layer
No schema validation — inter-crate data contracts (LLM request/response, MCP tool call, NATS envelope) are serde structs with no JSON-Schema or protobuf anchor
No e2e harness — integration tests are feature-gated per-crate; no docker-compose spanning NATS+SurrealDB+MinIO+MCP
No per-crate CHANGELOG — cliff.toml present (24 lines) but root CHANGELOG.md only has 1 Unreleased entry; consumers can't see per-crate deltas
No auto-instrumentation hooks — phenotype-mcp-server has no #[auto-instrument] for tool handlers; phenotype-llm has no token/cost span attributes
No rate limiting — public adapter APIs (e.g. pheno-nats publish, pheno-minio put) have no token-bucket or per-key QPS guard
No MCP server tooling split — phenotype-mcp-server is hosted here but NFR-OBS-010 says MCP server crates should live in PhenoMCP (domain squat)
Empty crates/phenotype-surrealdb + pheno-minio — only src/lib.rs stubs; no real SurrealDB/MinIO client implementation
No WASM/skill surface — README and CLAUDE.md mention 'daemon, skills, WASM' but only the daemon binary stub exists; no wasm32 target, no skill host
convergence_rationale: Rust-core multi-crate workspace (5 adapters) with cross-ecosystem metadata (go.mod, package.json, pyproject.toml, bun.lock) for org-wide dependency scanning. Architectural intent is to be the runtime substrate for the entire Phenotype polyrepo, but the binary and 2 of 5 adapter crates are still stubs — production weight is concentrated in phenotype-llm + pheno-nats + phenotype-mcp-server. Multi-stack by surface, with a convergence trajectory toward 'the' runtime layer.

action_items.title: Replace src/main.rs:1 placeholder with a real daemon entrypoint (config load + signal handling + adapter bootstrap)
action_items.why: The crate is named pheno-runtime and CLAUDE.md promises a daemon surface, but the binary is a one-liner. Without a real entrypoint the workspace cannot be consumed as a daemon and downstream consumers (PhenoAgent, PhenoProc) must hand-roll their own bootstrap.

action_items.title: Adopt phenotype-port-interfaces from phenoShared across all 5 adapter crates and delete duplicated trait definitions
action_items.why: README:91 promises 'hexagonal alignment' and ARCHITECTURE.md:5 repeats the claim, but no crate declares phenotype-port-interfaces in its Cargo.toml. Mainlining the org ports crate removes the trait duplication and makes phenoRuntime a true adapter layer.

action_items.title: Add OpenTelemetry tracing + retry/circuit-breaker post-processor to all adapters via phenotype-observably-* or phenotype-postfx
action_items.why: Two SOTA gaps (no tracing, no resilience) share a single wrapper layer (#[instrument] + tower/retry middleware). Building them together is cheaper than two passes and unifies the telemetry story across the org.

action_items.title: Wire release-plz (or cargo-release) to publish each adapter crate independently with provenance attestation
action_items.why: release.yml exists but does not call cargo publish. Independent per-crate versioning unblocks downstream repos that currently pin to a git SHA (per README:60-67). Adds crates.io provenance + SBOM for free.

action_items.title: Move phenotype-mcp-server out of phenoRuntime to PhenoMCP (resolve domain-squat per NFR-OBS-010)
action_items.why: The MCP server is a domain application, not a runtime adapter; per NFR-OBS-010 it belongs in PhenoMCP. Moving it cleans the workspace's stated scope ('runtime substrate + adapters') and removes a directory-level conflict with the same crate name in PhenoObservability.
